design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse side-scrolling levels using sword combat, platforming, and secondary weapons; defeat boss encounters at act conclusions; manage limited lives and timer pressure
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Execution difficulty combined with pattern-based enemy encounters; time pressure via level timer; resource scarcity (lives, ninpo for special weapons)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody a skilled ninja avenging his father; master fluid sword combat and wall-climbing; progress through cinematic narrative involving global conspiracies and demonic forces
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising difficulty curve that escalates significantly in later acts; tight execution requirements favor precision over player agency; narrative delivered through elaborate anime-style cutscenes rather than in-level storytelling; secondary weapons and special moves require resource management (ninpo points), forcing strategic decision-making

difficulty.lives_system: Three lives per continue; game over when all lives depleted; continues available to retry at level start
difficulty.continues_system: Player may continue from the level where game over occurred; unlimited continues available
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-level checkpoints; failure restarts entire level; acts comprise multiple levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal; difficulty is fixed and scales sharply between acts. Player has no difficulty settings or assist modes. Secondary weapon selection (Fire Wheel, Throwing Stars, Windmill Stars, Jump and Slash) provides tactical options but limited mitigation of core challenge

progression.upgrade_system: Secondary weapon acquisition via pickups (Fire Wheel, Throwing Stars, Windmill Stars, Jump and Slash, Invincible Fire Wheel); ninpo point collection for special attack resource; no permanent character progression between levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential act and level structure; progression is mandatory—no branching or optional content; each act builds on previous mechanical and narrative foundations
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select accessible via Game Genie codes
Sound test mode for music/audio content
Speed running potential via optimized routing and enemy manipulation
Varied secondary weapon choices create tactical replay value

world_building_techniques: Elaborate anime-style cinematic cutscenes between acts revealing plot progression
Character dialogue exposition (Ken's letter, Dr. Smith exposition, Foster briefing, Malth deathbed reveal)
Act-specific title cards in Japanese version (Destiny, Escape, Pursuit, Trap, Life-or-death Struggle, Conquering the Devil) framing thematic narrative beats
Environmental context: temple exploration, jungle parachute drop, demon statue mechanics tied to lunar eclipse climax
Character arcs: father's control reversal, Irene's betrayal redemption, Jaquio's apocalyptic scheme

boss_design.philosophy: Each boss represents a narrative antagonist (Malice Four members plus Jashin final form); bosses test pattern recognition and execution precision; encounters escalate in complexity and aggression across acts; each boss functions as narrative climax
notable_bosses: Basaquer: statue thief encounter early game
Bloody Malth: reveals father's survival, progression gating
Masked Devil (Ken): narrative twist encounter with controlled father
The Jaquio: primary antagonist, fiery projectile attacks
Jashin: final demon form triggered by lunar eclipse, ultimate challenge

faction_indicators: The Jaquio's Guardia de Mieux: antagonist organization seeking world domination through demon awakening
CIA Special Auxiliary Unit (Foster's faction): government agency monitoring demonic threats, betrays Ryu via assassination order
Ken Hayabusa's ninja clan: traditionalist discipline (father's sealing of temple, code of honor)
Irene Lew: initially Foster's agent, switches allegiance to Ryu, represents redemption through personal connection
danger_signals: Demon statues as weapons of mass destruction (Jashin's apocalyptic potential)
2000-year-old sealed temple as containment site: failure equals global catastrophe
Jaquio's lunar eclipse timing: calculated supernatural event coordination
Ken's possession/control: infiltration of ninja leadership, puppet mastery
Foster's treachery: institutional corruption despite official anti-demon mandate
environmental_storytelling: Amazon temple ruins: ancient sealed evil, archaeological evidence of prior containment
Catacomb descent: underworld journey mirroring spiritual/moral descent into darkness
Temple architecture progression: ascent through layers toward inner chambers and truth
Lunar eclipse visual: cosmic event marking supernatural unleashing, temporal urgency
Collapsing temple finale: environment as consequence of protagonist victory, father's sacrifice
